back_translation: And the Philistines gathered all their camps at Aphek, and Israel was encamping at the spring that is in Jezreel.
And the leaders of the Philistines were passing by in hundreds and in thousands, and David and his men were passing by in the rear with Achish.

And the commanders of the Philistines said, 'What are these Hebrews?' And Achish said to the commanders of the Philistines, 'Is not this David, the servant of Saul king of Israel, who has been with me these days or these years, and I have not found in him anything from the day of his falling away until this day?'

And the commanders of the Philistines were angry with him, and the commanders of the Philistines said to him, 'Send the man back, and let him return to his place which you have assigned him, and he shall not go down with us into battle, lest he become an adversary to us in battle. And by what could this one make himself acceptable to his lord — is it not by the heads of these men?
Is not this David, of whom they would sing in the dances, saying, Saul has struck down his thousands, and David his ten thousands?'

And Achish called David and said to him, 'As the LORD lives, you are upright, and your going out and coming in with me in the camp is good in my eyes, for I have not found in you evil from the day of your coming to me until this day. But in the eyes of the leaders, you are not good.
And now, return and go in peace, and do not do evil in the eyes of the leaders of the Philistines.'

And David said to Achish, 'But what have I done, and what have you found in your servant from the day that I have been before you until this day, that I should not come and fight against the enemies of my lord the king?'
And Achish answered and said to David, 'I know that you are good in my eyes like an angel of God; only the commanders of the Philistines have said, He shall not go up with us into battle.
And now, rise early in the morning, with the servants of your lord who came with you. And rise early in the morning, and when there is light for you, go.'
And David rose early, he and his men, to go in the morning, to return to the land of the Philistines. And the Philistines went up to Jezreel.
